Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. C64: Putting Sprite Multiplexing to Work 26 upvotes, 0 comments
  2. The Xkcd thing, now interactive 396 upvotes, 47 comments
  3. Guido van Rossum Interviews Thomas Wouters (Python Core Dev) 67 upvotes, 5 comments
  4. First in-utero stem cell therapy for fetal spina bifida repair is safe: study 324 upvotes, 62 comments
  5. Physicists developing a quantum computer that’s entirely open source 149 upvotes, 27 comments
  6. I'm losing the SEO battle for my own open source project 43 upvotes, 5 comments
  7. How to sew a Hyperbolic Blanket (2021) 19 upvotes, 1 comments
  8. Show HN: I built a sub-500ms latency voice agent from scratch 480 upvotes, 137 comments
  9. Simple screw counter 190 upvotes, 51 comments
  10. Motorola announces a partnership with GrapheneOS 2248 upvotes, 818 comments
  11. DOS Memory Management 78 upvotes, 22 comments
  12. I built a pint-sized Macintosh 57 upvotes, 16 comments
  13. Launch HN: OctaPulse (YC W26) – Robotics and computer vision for fish farming 103 upvotes, 37 comments
  14. British Columbia is permanently adopting daylight time 931 upvotes, 454 comments
  15. Arm's Cortex X925: Reaching Desktop Performance 155 upvotes, 73 comments
  16. Meta’s AI smart glasses and data privacy concerns 1209 upvotes, 698 comments
  17. India's top court angry after junior judge cites fake AI-generated orders 101 upvotes, 46 comments
  18. We Built a Video Rendering Engine by Lying to the Browser About What Time It Is 84 upvotes, 39 comments
  19. Computer Says No 16 upvotes, 4 comments
  20. Mullvad VPN: Banned TV Ad in the Streets of London [video] 105 upvotes, 54 comments
  21. Privacy-preserving age and identity verification via anonymous credentials 52 upvotes, 23 comments
  22. Ars Technica fires reporter after AI controversy involving fabricated quotes 404 upvotes, 252 comments
  23. The Internet's Top Tech Publications Lost 58% of Their Google Traffic Since 2024 5 upvotes, 0 comments
  24. Stolen Gemini API key racks up $82,000 in 48 hours 71 upvotes, 40 comments
  25. Buckle Up for Bumpier Skies 59 upvotes, 32 comments
  26. Guilty Displeasures 94 upvotes, 95 comments
  27. The beauty and terror of modding Windows 52 upvotes, 57 comments
  28. iPhone 17e 306 upvotes, 447 comments
  29. New iPad Air, powered by M4 419 upvotes, 651 comments
  30. We Automated Everything Except Knowing What's Going On 22 upvotes, 23 comments

Last generated 10:00pm, 3 March 2026. Source here.
